meta.purpose: Static health context for Randy Daems — loaded into coach system prompt. Update when labs, meds, or supplements change.

profile.lifestyle: Working professional with family and kids. Cannot follow extreme protocols like Bryan Johnson's (8:30 PM bedtime, noon last meal, full-day medical schedule). Will adopt evidence-based principles that fit a working parent's life.
profile.annual_goal: 50-mile mountain hiking trek — train for endurance and lower-body strength

hashimotos_risk.status: Active autoimmune concern. TPOAb elevated at 194 IU/mL (ref: 0–34). Thyroid hormones (TSH, Free T3, Free T4) currently within normal range. Goal: reduce TPOAb through interventions before thyroid function is affected.
current_interventions: Gluten-free diet — started approx April 2026, doctor-recommended, MEDICAL not preference
Vitamin D3 + K2 (successfully improved D from 25.5 to 51.1 ng/mL)
hashimotos_risk.next_lab_retest: A few weeks from 2026-05-26 — first real signal on whether TPOAb is responding
coach_rules: NEVER suggest gluten as acceptable. Gluten-free is a medical intervention, not a dietary preference.
Track TPOAb trend as the primary Hashimoto's marker. Lab value alone is not enough — direction of change matters.
Oura HRV and sleep data are useful recovery proxies but cannot confirm or deny Hashimoto's status.
Anti-inflammatory food priorities directly support autoimmune reduction: EVOO, berries, fermented foods, cruciferous vegetables, omega-3.

kidney_context.status: Creatinine elevated on most recent labs. eGFR 51 is below normal (60+). Context: high activity level, muscle mass, and possible dehydration at draw time can elevate creatinine. Creatine supplementation STOPPED because of this.
coach_rules: Do NOT recommend creatine — Randy stopped it due to elevated creatinine/eGFR.
Monitor protein intake in context of kidney function — flag if protein targets seem aggressive relative to eGFR.
Note hydration as relevant to creatinine interpretation.
Flag any further eGFR decline at next labs as requiring clinician review.

glucose_triglycerides_flag.fasting_status: LIKELY FASTING — Randy now recalls he was likely fasting for Lab Set 2. Lab Set 1 (Dec 2025) was confirmed NOT fasting.
glucose_triglycerides_flag.interpretation: Fasting glucose 129 mg/dL exceeds the diabetes diagnostic threshold of 126 mg/dL. A single reading is not a diagnosis, but this is clinically significant and requires a confirmatory fasting retest. Fasting triglycerides 182 mg/dL is borderline high (normal <150, borderline 150–199, high 200+). HbA1c has never been tested — this is the most important single addition to the next labs. Randy will be confirmed fasting for his upcoming third labs.
coach_rules: Flag glucose as potentially at the pre-diabetes/diabetes threshold — next labs will be the first fasting-confirmed retest.
Do not alarm or diagnose. Frame as: this warrants a confirmed retest, which is happening soon.
HbA1c is the priority new marker — it gives 90-day glucose average, unaffected by fasting status.
Triglycerides respond strongly to dietary carbohydrate intake and training load — contextualize against Randy's high-carb fueling strategy before over-interpreting.

wellbutrin_bupropion.coach_context: Randy is open to reducing or eventually eliminating Wellbutrin if lifestyle interventions support mood stabilization. He will NOT pursue extreme interventions (e.g., psychedelic therapy). Path forward is through sleep optimization, exercise consistency, inflammation reduction, and Vitamin D adequacy — all already in the TrainAI protocol. NEVER suggest medication changes. Frame lifestyle improvements as supporting mental resilience. Any medication changes are conversations with his doctor.

eszopiclone_lunesta.pattern: 2mg at bedtime most nights. Sometimes an additional 1mg if waking during the night (e.g., 4 AM). Maximum total = 3mg. Goal is to reduce reliance as natural sleep quality improves.
eszopiclone_lunesta.coach_context: Eszopiclone (Lunesta) is a sedative-hypnotic. The extra 1mg is reactive to night waking — not a fixed dose. As sleep quality improves through magnesium, consistent bedtime, reduced LDN disruption, and lower inflammation, the need for the extra dose should decrease. Acknowledge nights where the extra dose was not needed as a positive trend.
eszopiclone_lunesta.tracking: Bedtime dose (mg) + whether extra dose was taken during night + time of extra dose

ldn_low_dose_naltrexone.current_issue: 4.5mg at night may be too stimulating and causing sleep disruption. Lower dose or morning timing being considered with clinician.
ldn_low_dose_naltrexone.tracking: Dose mg + time taken + sleep quality that night + wakeups + next-day energy
coach_rules: If Oura shows disrupted sleep on nights LDN was taken, flag this pattern for clinician discussion.
Do not recommend dose changes — these are clinician decisions.
Track LDN timing vs sleep HRV and efficiency to surface patterns.

supplements.tracking_note: Randy takes supplements 60-70% of the time. Simple daily checklist is more likely to be completed than detailed logging.

morning_daily_advantage.label: Daily Advantage
morning_daily_advantage.known_contents: Multi-supplement morning pack (exact contents to be confirmed)

nighttime_supplements.label: Nighttime Supplements
nighttime_supplements.note: Eszopiclone is tracked separately under medications.

creatine.reason: Elevated creatinine on last labs (1.61 mg/dL, eGFR 51). Stopped until kidney function is clarified at next labs.
coach_rules: Do not recommend creatine. Do not suggest resuming without clear eGFR improvement and clinician guidance.

gluten_free.status: STRICT — medical intervention for Hashimoto's, not preference
coach_rules: Gluten-free is non-negotiable. Never suggest gluten-containing foods. Flag any recipe or food suggestion for GF compliance.

dairy.status: Mostly dairy-free / low lactose due to lactose intolerance. Some hard cheese tolerated.
coach_rules: Avoid dairy recommendations. Hard aged cheeses acceptable in small amounts.

thyroid.interpretation: Thyroid function normal. Autoimmune activity elevated and stable. Next retest in a few weeks will show first signal from LDN + gluten-free intervention.

vitamins.interpretation: Major improvement. Now at low end of optimal range (50-80 ng/mL target). Continue D3+K2.

kidney.interpretation: Below normal. Creatine stopped. Confirm with fasting retest and inform clinician.

hormones.interpretation: Total testosterone stable. Free testosterone improved significantly (7.3 → 9.7) — likely related to Vitamin D optimization.

inflammation.hsCRP: not yet tested — HIGH PRIORITY for next labs

labs_next_priorities: hsCRP — inflammation baseline (critical for anti-aging protocol)
eGFR / creatinine — track kidney function trend
TgAb — second thyroid autoimmune marker not yet tested

assumed_daily_habits.note: These are consistent behaviors Randy does reliably but does not log. The coach should treat them as present unless there is reason to think otherwise. Do not flag these as missing data.
habits: LMNT electrolytes — always taken on workout/gym days. Not tracked per-day.
Chia seeds + hemp hearts + flax seeds — added to protein shakes. Regular habit, not logged.
Olive oil — used regularly in cooking. Not tracked per meal.
Protein shakes — regular part of diet, not always logged individually.
no_tracking: Water intake — Randy does not track water and has no plans to. Do not ask about or reference water logging.
Every micro-ingredient in shakes and meals — some nutritional context will always be invisible. The macro totals from MacrosFirst are the best available signal.

protocol_realism.context: Randy works in an office, has a family with kids, and cannot follow extreme protocols. The Bryan Johnson framework is a source of evidence-based principles, not a prescription to follow exactly.

practical_adaptations.last_meal_time: Target 7–8 PM (not noon). Gives 2-3 hours before a realistic 10–10:30 PM bedtime.
practical_adaptations.morning_light: 10–15 min walk or outdoor time within 30 min of waking — achievable before work
practical_adaptations.sauna: 2–3x/week post-VASA workout if available — not daily
practical_adaptations.screen_wind_down: 30–45 min before bed — realistic with family life
practical_adaptations.exercise: Already meeting or exceeding 6h/week protocol target
practical_adaptations.anti_inflammatory_foods: Incorporate EVOO, berries, fermented foods into existing meals — no lifestyle overhaul needed

coach_rules: Never recommend Bryan Johnson's exact schedule. Extract the principle, apply to real life.
Account for family and work constraints when suggesting behavior changes.
Frame recommendations as additions or swaps, not wholesale lifestyle overhauls.
Prioritize interventions with the highest evidence-to-effort ratio for someone with Randy's specific health profile.
